title: OpenArm per-episode capture-timestamp sidecar (CTR-CAP@v1)
description: The per-episode sidecar that carries each camera frame's grab-time capture instant, joined to the LeRobot dataset by frame_index. Every time-, camera- and queue-shaped element is consumed from CTR-PRIM@v1 and not restated (02b §5.0b). This is the language-agnostic mirror of contracts/capture/schema.py; the two must not diverge. Frozen by content hash at WP-3A-06; DRAFT until then.

episode_index.description: The episode this sidecar belongs to; constant within one sidecar.
episode_index.name: episode_index

frame_index.description: The contiguous 0-based frame position; the sidecar-to-dataset join key.
frame_index.name: frame_index

capture_ts.description: Per-slot grab instant. Column stem consumed from CTR-PRIM's CAPTURE_TS_COLUMN_SUFFIX; a CLOCK_MONOTONIC nanosecond CaptureTimestamp attached immediately after grab.

sensor_ts.description: Optional per-slot device hardware timestamp, preserved in parallel beside capture_ts, never in place of it.

frame_number.description: Optional per-slot device frame counter, preserved in parallel for continuity checks.

attachment_site.description: capture_ts is produced at the grab site and nowhere else. A consumer that stamps receive time under the capture column is the FAIL_BLOCKING defect (02b §5.2 WP-3A-02 ①); the static ban in contracts/capture/attachment.py catches it.

lerobot_timestamp.description: The LeRobot dataset 'timestamp' column is frame_index/fps: a synthetic playback grid in seconds, orthogonal to when a frame was captured. Marked synthetic for meta/UI so it is never read as a real capture time (02b §5.2 WP-3A-02 ④).

capture_match_queue.description: The queue class a capture-to-frame match uses, consumed from CTR-PRIM QUEUE_PROFILES. A match miss is a counted drop, not a defect; a frame that cannot be matched is dropped and tallied, never interpolated or duplicated.

verifiers.description: The checks WP-3B-04 runs over real capture data (02b §5.2 WP-3A-02 ③).
verifiers.host_sensor_offset: capture_ts - sensor_ts, in ns, per frame where both are present
verifiers.offset_drift_span: peak-to-peak of the host-sensor offset series over the episode
verifiers.frame_number_continuity: the device frame counter increments by exactly one each kept step
